title: Annual exchange rates
summary: Bank of Canada publishes annual average exchange rates for 23 currencies expressed as CAD per unit of foreign currency. Data covers 2021-2025 and is published by 12:30 ET on the last business day of the year. These are indicative rates only, derived from aggregated price quotes from financial institutions.
key_points: Bank of Canada annual average exchange rates cover 23 currencies from 2021-2025
Rates are published by 12:30 ET on the last business day of the year
Exchange rates are expressed as 1 unit of foreign currency converted into Canadian dollars
US dollar appreciated from 1.2535 CAD in 2021 to 1.3978 CAD in 2025 (11.5% gain)
Euro strengthened significantly from 1.3696 CAD in 2022 to 1.5782 CAD in 2025
Japanese yen weakened considerably from 0.01142 CAD in 2021 to 0.00935 CAD in 2025
Chinese renminbi relatively stable around 0.19 CAD throughout the period
Swiss franc strengthened from 1.3713 CAD in 2021 to 1.6846 CAD in 2025
Turkish lira weakened substantially from 0.1450 CAD in 2021 to 0.03550 CAD in 2025
All rates are indicative only, obtained from averages of aggregated price quotes from financial institutions
why_it_matters: Exchange rates are fundamental macro catalysts affecting trade competitiveness, import/export costs, inflation dynamics, and monetary policy decisions. The USD/CAD and EUR/CAD trends are particularly important for Canadian trade and tourism sectors.
macro_context: The data shows significant CAD depreciation against major currencies over 2021-2025, with the USD strengthening 11.5% and EUR 11.4% over the period. The Swiss franc appreciated 22.8% against CAD. These trends reflect broader CAD weakness influencing Canadian import costs and export competitiveness.
notes: Data release page containing historical annual average exchange rates. Rates are indicative and sourced from financial institution quotes. The published_at date is inferred as 2025-12-31 based on the data coverage and stated publication timing (last business day of year).
